questions.text: What does 'An-Nasr' mean?
questions.answer: The Help or The Divine Support.

questions.text: What are the two things mentioned in the first verse?
questions.answer: The Help of Allah (Nasrullah) and the Victory/Conquest (Fath).

questions.text: What 'Conquest' does the Surah refer to?
questions.answer: The Conquest of Makkah (Fath Makkah).

questions.text: What will the Prophet see the people doing in Verse 2?
questions.answer: Entering the religion of Allah in crowds (Afwaja).

questions.text: What three things is the Prophet commanded to do when victory comes?
questions.answer: Glorify (Tasbih), Praise (Hamd), and Ask for Forgiveness (Istighfar).

questions.text: How is Allah described in the final verse?
questions.answer: As the 'At-Tawwab' (The Accepter of Repentance).

questions.text: What was the deeper meaning of this Surah regarding the Prophet's life?
questions.answer: It was a sign that his mission was complete and his death was near.

questions.text: Who correctly understood the sign of the Prophet's approaching death from this Surah?
questions.answer: Ibn Abbas (RA) and Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA).

questions.text: Is this a Makkan or Madinan Surah?
questions.answer: Madinan (it was one of the last Surahs revealed).

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah An-Nasr?
questions.answer: Three verses.

questions.text: What does 'Afwaja' mean?
questions.answer: In crowds / in multitudes.

questions.text: Why is the Prophet told to ask for forgiveness after a great victory?
questions.answer: To maintain humility, acknowledge that victory is from Allah alone, and to set an example for the Ummah.

questions.text: What did the Prophet (SAW) say frequently in his Ruku and Sajdah after this Surah?
questions.answer: 'Subhanaka Allahumma Rabbana wa bihamdika, Allahumma-ghfirli'.

questions.text: Does 'Nasrullah' apply only to Fath Makkah?
questions.answer: It applied specifically then, but it is a universal concept of Allah's support for the truth.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Sab-bih'?
questions.answer: Glorify / Exalt.

questions.text: What is the significance of Allah being 'Tawwab'?
questions.answer: He is always ready to accept the repentance of those who turn to Him.

questions.text: What happened to the Arab tribes after the conquest of Makkah?
questions.answer: They realized the truth of Islam and sent delegations from all over Arabia to pledge allegiance.

questions.text: True or False: Surah An-Nasr is the final Surah of the Quran in terms of its position.
questions.answer: False, it is the 110th.

questions.text: What is the relationship between 'Praise' and 'Forgiveness' in this Surah?
questions.answer: Praise is for the blessing of victory, and forgiveness is for any human shortcoming in the service of Allah.

questions.text: How does the Surah conclude?
questions.answer: With the assurance that Allah accepts repentance.
